back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,
Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: A man, when there is a discharge from his flesh, his discharge is unclean.
And this shall be his uncleanness in his discharge: his flesh runs his discharge, or his flesh blocks his discharge—his uncleanness it is.
Every bed on which the one with a discharge lies shall be unclean, and every furniture on which he sits shall be unclean.
And a man who touches his bed shall wash his clothes and bathe in water, and be unclean until evening.
And the one who sits on the furniture on which the one with the discharge sits shall wash his clothes and bathe in water, and be unclean until evening.
And the one who touches the flesh of the one with the discharge shall wash his clothes and bathe in water, and be unclean until evening.
And when the one with the discharge spits on the clean one, he shall wash his clothes and bathe in water, and be unclean until evening.
And every saddle on which the one with the discharge rides shall be unclean.
And every one who touches anything that was under him shall be unclean until evening; and the one who carries them shall wash his clothes and bathe in water, and be unclean until evening.
And every one whom the one with the discharge touches, when he has not rinsed his hands in water, shall wash his clothes and bathe in water, and be unclean until evening.
And a clay vessel that the one with the discharge touches shall be broken, and every wooden vessel shall be rinsed in water.

And when the one with the discharge is cleansed from his discharge, he shall count for himself seven days for his cleansing, and wash his clothes, and bathe his flesh in fresh water, and he shall be clean.
And on the eighth day he shall take for himself two turtledoves or two young pigeons, and come before the face of the Lord, to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and give them to the priest.
And the priest shall offer them, one as a sin offering and one as a burnt offering. And the priest shall make atonement for him before the face of the Lord from his discharge.
And a man, when there goes out from him an emission of seed, he shall bathe in water all his flesh, and be unclean until evening.
And every garment and every leather on which there is an emission of seed shall be washed in water, and be unclean until evening.
And a woman with whom a man lies an emission of seed, they shall bathe in water and be unclean until evening.
And a woman, when there is a discharge of blood in her flesh, seven days she shall be in her menstruation, and every one who touches her shall be unclean until evening.
And everything she lies on in her menstruation shall be unclean, and everything she sits on shall be unclean.
And every one who touches her bed shall wash his clothes and bathe in water, and be unclean until evening.
And every one who touches any furniture on which she sits shall wash his clothes and bathe in water, and be unclean until evening.
And if on the bed it is, or on the furniture on which she sits, when he touches it, he shall be unclean until evening.
And if a man indeed lies with her and her menstruation is on him, he shall be unclean seven days, and every bed on which he lies shall be unclean.

And a woman, when her discharge of blood flows many days outside the time of her menstruation, or when it flows beyond her menstruation, all the days of her unclean discharge, like the days of her menstruation, she shall be unclean.

Every bed on which she lies all the days of her discharge shall be to her like the bed of her menstruation, and every furniture on which she sits shall be unclean, like the uncleanness of her menstruation.
And every one who touches them shall be unclean, and shall wash his clothes and bathe in water, and be unclean until evening.
And if she is cleansed from her discharge, she shall count for herself seven days, and afterward she shall be clean.
And on the eighth day she shall take for herself two turtledoves or two young pigeons, and bring them to the priest, to the entrance of the tent of meeting.
And the priest shall offer one as a sin offering and one as a burnt offering. And the priest shall make atonement for her before the face of the Lord from her unclean discharge.
And you shall separate the sons of Israel from their uncleanness, that they may not die in their uncleanness when they defile My tabernacle that is in their midst.
This is the law of the one with a discharge, and of him from whom goes out an emission of seed, to be defiled by it.
And of the woman suffering in her menstruation, and of the one with a discharge of his discharge—for the male and for the female—and for a man who lies with an unclean woman.
